dns.business_impact: Domain does not resolve. All web traffic, email delivery, and API integrations are completely offline. Customers cannot reach your website or services.
dns.recommendation: Verify DNS zone configuration with your registrar. Ensure the domain has not expired and nameservers are correctly delegated.

dns.business_impact: DNS server failure indicates authoritative nameservers are unreachable or misconfigured. Intermittent outages will degrade user trust and harm SEO rankings.
dns.recommendation: Check nameserver health. Consider migrating to a managed DNS provider with built-in redundancy (e.g., Cloudflare, AWS Route53).

dns.business_impact: Email delivery is broken if this happens for MX, or no web traffic if A record. Inbound messages will bounce, impacting customer communication.
dns.recommendation: Configure the necessary DNS records (MX, A, etc.) pointing to your provider.

dns.business_impact: Without an SPF record, email from your domain is vulnerable to spoofing. This increases phishing risk and may cause legitimate emails to land in spam folders, reducing marketing campaign effectiveness.
dns.recommendation: Add a TXT record with a valid SPF policy (e.g., v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all).

dns.business_impact: Missing DMARC policy means you have no visibility into email authentication failures and no enforcement against spoofing. This is a compliance gap for industries requiring email security standards.
dns.recommendation: Publish a DMARC TXT record at _dmarc.yourdomain.com starting with p=none for monitoring, then escalate to p=quarantine or p=reject.

dns.business_impact: Multiple A records may indicate round-robin DNS load balancing, which is acceptable. However, verify all IPs are active to avoid sending users to dead endpoints.
dns.recommendation: Confirm all listed IP addresses are serving content. Remove stale records.

ssl.business_impact: Expired SSL certificate triggers browser security warnings that block visitors. Conversion rates drop drastically as users perceive the site as unsafe. Google will also demote the site in search rankings.
ssl.recommendation: Renew the certificate immediately. Enable auto-renewal via your CA or use Let's Encrypt with certbot for automated management.

ssl.business_impact: Self-signed certificates are not trusted by browsers. Visitors will see a full-page security warning, destroying trust and causing near-total traffic loss.
ssl.recommendation: Replace with a certificate from a trusted Certificate Authority. Free options include Let's Encrypt.

ssl.business_impact: Certificate expiring within 30 days poses an imminent risk of outage. If renewal fails, the site will display security errors, halting all secure transactions.
ssl.recommendation: Renew the certificate now. Set up monitoring alerts for certificate expiration dates.

ssl.business_impact: TLS 1.0 is deprecated and vulnerable to known attacks (BEAST, POODLE). PCI DSS compliance requires TLS 1.2 minimum. Using outdated protocols may result in audit failures and data breach liability.
ssl.recommendation: Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 on the server. Configure TLS 1.2 as the minimum supported version, with TLS 1.3 preferred.

ssl.business_impact: TLS 1.1 is deprecated. Major browsers no longer support it, meaning some users may be unable to connect. This also fails PCI DSS compliance requirements.
ssl.recommendation: Upgrade server configuration to require TLS 1.2 or higher.

ssl.business_impact: Certificate hostname mismatch causes browsers to reject the connection entirely. Users cannot access the site, and automated systems (APIs, webhooks) will fail SSL verification.
ssl.recommendation: Reissue the certificate with the correct Subject Alternative Names (SANs) covering all domains and subdomains in use.

http.business_impact: Without HTTP Strict Transport Security, users may connect over insecure HTTP on first visit, exposing session cookies and credentials to man-in-the-middle attacks.
http.recommendation: Add the Strict-Transport-Security header with a max-age of at least 31536000 (1 year). Consider HSTS preloading.

http.business_impact: Missing X-Frame-Options header allows the site to be embedded in iframes on malicious domains, enabling clickjacking attacks that can steal user credentials or trigger unwanted actions.
http.recommendation: Set X-Frame-Options to DENY or SAMEORIGIN. Alternatively, use Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors directive.

http.business_impact: Without CSP, the site is vulnerable to cross-site scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. A successful attack can compromise user data, deface the site, and damage brand reputation.
http.recommendation: Implement a Content-Security-Policy header. Start with report-only mode to identify violations before enforcing.

http.business_impact: Missing fundamental security headers leaves the site highly exposed to various attacks including XSS, Clickjacking, and MIME-sniffing exploits.
http.recommendation: Implement a comprehensive security header posture (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options).

http.business_impact: Exposing the server software and version provides attackers with reconnaissance data to target known vulnerabilities specific to that software version.
http.recommendation: Remove or obfuscate the Server header in your web server configuration.

http.business_impact: The X-Powered-By header reveals the backend technology stack, making it easier for attackers to craft targeted exploits.
http.recommendation: Remove the X-Powered-By header. In Express.js, use app.disable('x-powered-by'). In PHP, set expose_php = Off.

http.business_impact: Permanent redirect detected. Ensure redirect chains are minimal (ideally one hop) to preserve SEO link equity and reduce page load time.
http.recommendation: Audit redirect chains. Update internal links and canonical URLs to point directly to final destinations.

http.business_impact: Temporary redirect detected. If this should be permanent, using 302 instead of 301 prevents search engines from transferring link equity to the destination URL, weakening SEO.
http.recommendation: If the redirect is permanent, change to a 301. Reserve 302 for genuinely temporary redirects.

http.business_impact: Page not found. Broken links degrade user experience, increase bounce rate, and waste crawl budget. Accumulated 404 errors signal poor site maintenance to search engines.
http.recommendation: Fix or redirect broken URLs. Implement a custom 404 page with navigation to help users find relevant content.

http.business_impact: Internal server error indicates a backend crash or misconfiguration. Users cannot complete transactions, and repeated 500 errors will cause Google to deindex affected pages.
http.recommendation: Check server logs immediately. Common causes: application exceptions, database connection failures, or resource exhaustion.

http.business_impact: Service unavailable. The server is overloaded or under maintenance. Prolonged 503 status will trigger search engine deindexing and customer churn.
http.recommendation: If planned maintenance, ensure Retry-After header is set. If unexpected, investigate server load, scaling, and upstream dependencies.

whois.business_impact: Domain expiring within 30 days risks complete loss of the domain name. If it lapses, competitors or squatters can register it, causing catastrophic brand damage and SEO loss.
whois.recommendation: Renew the domain immediately. Enable auto-renewal and ensure registrar payment methods are up to date.

whois.business_impact: Newly registered domains (under 6 months) often have lower trust scores with search engines. This can result in slower SEO indexing and reduced email deliverability during the initial period.
whois.recommendation: Focus on building domain authority through quality backlinks and consistent content publishing. Ensure all DNS and email authentication records are properly configured from day one.

whois.business_impact: WHOIS privacy protection hides registrant details, which is standard practice. However, for business domains, having verifiable contact information can increase trust with partners and customers.
whois.recommendation: No action required for most cases. For B2B organizations, consider whether transparent WHOIS data adds credibility.
